clues.question: What is the capital of France?
clues.explainer: Paris has been France's capital for over a thousand years.

clues.question: What is the capital of Japan?
clues.explainer: Tokyo became the capital in 1868, replacing Kyoto.

clues.question: What is the capital of Canada?
clues.explainer: Ottawa was chosen as a compromise between English and French Canada.

clues.question: What is the capital of Australia?
clues.explainer: Canberra was purpose-built as the capital in 1913.

clues.question: What is the capital of Kazakhstan?
clues.explainer: Astana (briefly renamed Nur-Sultan) became the capital in 1997.

clues.question: Which animal is the largest on Earth?
clues.explainer: Blue whales can grow to nearly 30 metres long.

clues.question: How many legs does a spider have?
clues.explainer: Eight legs is a defining feature of arachnids.

clues.question: Which bird is famous for being unable to fly and living in Antarctica?
clues.explainer: Penguins are flightless seabirds adapted to swimming.

clues.question: What is a group of lions called?
clues.explainer: Lions live in family groups called prides.

clues.question: Which mammal is the only one capable of true sustained flight?
clues.explainer: Bats truly fly; the others only glide.

clues.question: Which movie features a young wizard named Harry attending Hogwarts?
clues.explainer: The Harry Potter films are based on J.K. Rowling's books.

clues.question: In which movie does a clownfish search the ocean for his lost son?
clues.explainer: Finding Nemo is a 2003 Pixar film.

clues.question: Which film series features the character Iron Man?
clues.explainer: Iron Man helped launch the Marvel Cinematic Universe in 2008.

clues.question: Who directed the 1997 film 'Titanic'?
clues.explainer: James Cameron also directed Avatar.

clues.question: Which 2019 film became the highest-grossing of all time at release?
clues.explainer: Avengers: Endgame briefly overtook Avatar at the global box office.

clues.question: What gas do plants absorb from the air to make food?
clues.explainer: Plants use carbon dioxide in photosynthesis.

clues.question: What is the closest planet to the Sun?
clues.explainer: Mercury orbits nearest the Sun.

clues.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
clues.explainer: Au comes from the Latin 'aurum'.

clues.question: What force keeps us anchored to the ground?
clues.explainer: Gravity pulls objects with mass toward each other.

clues.question: How many bones are in the adult human body?
clues.explainer: Adults have 206 bones; babies are born with more that fuse over time.

clues.question: Which country is the birthplace of pizza and pasta?
clues.explainer: Both dishes are icons of Italian cuisine.

clues.question: What is the main ingredient in guacamole?
clues.explainer: Guacamole is a mashed avocado dip from Mexico.

clues.question: Sushi is a dish that originated in which country?
clues.explainer: Modern sushi developed in Japan.

clues.question: Which spice, gram for gram, is the most expensive in the world?
clues.explainer: Saffron is hand-harvested from crocus flowers.

clues.question: Feta is a cheese traditionally from which country?
clues.explainer: Feta has protected origin status in Greece.

clues.question: Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
clues.explainer: Neil Armstrong stepped onto the Moon in July 1969.

clues.question: What was the first widely used social network to reach a billion users?
clues.explainer: Facebook passed one billion users in 2012.

clues.question: Which country was the first to give women the right to vote nationally?
clues.explainer: New Zealand granted women's suffrage in 1893.

clues.question: Who was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize?
clues.explainer: Marie Curie won the Physics prize in 1903.

clues.question: What was the first commercially successful personal computer company?
clues.explainer: Apple's early machines helped launch the home-computer era.
